title: Mile Zero Anomaly
description:
Your car is a wreck. You’re alone on a mountain road at night. Great.

There’s a gas station a few miles away. You have to walk there, following the signs at every fork in the road. Simple, right?

Rule number one: don’t turn back.

If you turn back, you’ll end up walking in circles forever. Keep moving forward, read the signs, choose the right direction. That’s it.

Rule number two: don’t trust everything you see.

The further you go, the more things start to feel off. Contradictory directions, details out of place, signs that don’t seem quite right. Nothing obvious. Just little things. But on this road, the little things matter.

Spot the anomalies. Choose wisely. Reach the station.

Or walk forever and die.

Key features:

• A nighttime mountain road with a dense, oppressive thriller atmosphere

• Forks in the road with signs to read and interpret at every turn

• Visual anomalies hidden in the details that must be spotted before you take a wrong turn

• Tension built on doubt and isolation
description_short: Car breakdown, deserted road, the dead of night. The only option is to follow the signs to the gas station. But on this last mile, not everything is as it seems.
